bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. The opponent’s opening offer gives me the lowest value on every issue despite describing it as balanced, so I rely on the bid numbers rather than the message and counter with my strongest valid position.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making only a small concession on my lowest-weight issue. The opponent has repeated the all-valueD offer, which is very low utility for me, and their cooperative messages are not backed by numerical concessions, so I should not move far yet.

I am targeting a high utility bid (~0.947) because it is still very early at t=0.04 and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering my worst values while describing them as “win-win.” I will make a small concession only on my lowest-weight issueD and hold firm on the higher-weight issuesA, issueB, and issueC, while avoiding repeating my exact previous bid.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.89 because it is still very early at t=0.06, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent has only moved from all valueD to valueC on issueA, which improves my utility slightly but remains far below acceptable, and their “balanced/win-win” messages are not supported by the offer history. I will concede on lower-weight issueC while holding firm on the more important issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.87 because it is still very early at t=0.08, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent has only conceded on issueA from valueD to valueC while keeping the rest at valueD, so I will acknowledge movement by conceding further only on my lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on the higher-weight issues. Their messages claim balance and fairness, but the actual offers remain low-utility for me, so I rely on the bidding pattern rather than the cheap talk.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.814, since it is early at t=0.10 and there is no need to concede deeply yet. The opponent has only improved their offer from all valueD to valueC on issueA while holding the rest at valueD, so their “balanced” messages are not very persuasive compared with the actual low-utility bids. I will concede on lower-weight issues first, keeping issueA and issueB at my strongest values while moving issueC down from my last offer to avoid repeating exactly.

I am targeting a utility around 0.76, still strong for me while making an additional concession because the opponent has only improved issueA and continues to hold issueB/issueC/issueD at valueD. Since time is still early at t=0.12, I will hold firm on my highest-weight issues A and B, while conceding further on lower-weight issueC and already conceding issueD. Their “balanced/win-win” messages are cheap talk; the actual bids show limited concession concentrated only on issueA.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.73 because it is early in the negotiation, but the opponent has shown some real concession only on issueA while holding issueB-D at valueD. Their messages claim balance, but the numbers show they are mostly firm, so I will keep my highest-weight issueA at valueA and make a measured concession on issueB and issueC while leaving issueD at their preferred-looking valueD.

I am targeting a high but not maximal utility because it is still early (t=0.16), so there is no need to rush into accepting the opponent’s very low-utility offers. The opponent has made some concessions on issueA and issueB, but their latest bid is still only about 0.28 utility for me, so I will hold firm on my most important issueA while conceding more on lower-weight issues.

I am targeting a utility around 0.76, since it is still early at t=0.18 and the opponent’s latest offer is worse for me than their earlier issueA concession. Their messages claim balance, but the offer history shows they mostly hold valueD and have only shifted concessions between issueA and issueB, so I will concede more on lower-weight issueC and issueD while holding firm on the most valuable issues.

I targeted a still-positive utility around 0.68, improving on the opponent’s latest offer to me while not conceding all the way on the important issueB. At t=0.20 there is still no deadline pressure to accept their package, but their repeated insistence on issueC and issueD at valueD suggests those are the easiest concessions, while their recent move to issueA=valueA is worth preserving.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.68 because it is early in the negotiation, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s low-utility packages yet. The opponent’s actual offers, despite “balanced” language, mostly keep three issues at my worst value and only occasionally concede one issue, so I am holding firm on high-weight issueA and issueB while conceding on lower-weight issueC and issueD. To avoid repeating my exact prior bid, I am making a small movement on issueD while preserving my key priorities.

I am targeting a utility around 0.68–0.70, since we are still relatively early at t=0.24 and should not concede too heavily yet. The opponent’s offers have improved only modestly and inconsistently for me, with their latest bid still low utility, while their messages overstate fairness compared with the numbers. I will concede on the lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on high-weight issueA and keeping issueB at a moderate compromise.

I am targeting a utility around 0.65, making a modest concession at t=0.26 while still protecting high-weight issues. The opponent’s offers remain low for me overall, but they have shown movement on issueA and issueB, so I am testing a tradeoff: concede issueA to valueB while keeping issueB at my top value and leaving lower-weight issues more favorable to them. Their messages claim fairness, but the actual offers are still quite one-sided, so I am relying on the bidding pattern rather than the language.

I am targeting a utility around 0.68, which is still strong for me but includes concessions on the lower-weight issues C and D. At t=0.28 there is no need to rush to the opponent’s low-utility offers, especially since their latest bid regressed to mostly valueD and only improves issueD slightly. Their messages claim balance, but the actual offers remain heavily tilted toward valueD, so I will hold firm on issueA while offering valueB on issueB and conceding issueC/issueD.

I am targeting a utility around 0.70, since at t=0.30 there is still time and the opponent’s offers remain much lower for me despite occasional concessions. Their messages repeatedly frame offers as “balanced,” but the actual bids mostly keep issueB/issueC/issueD low for me, so I will hold firm on high-weight issueA and issueB while making concessions on lower-weight issues.

I am targeting a utility around 0.68–0.70, which is a meaningful concession but still preserves my highest-weight issueA at valueA. At t=0.32 there is some time pressure to move toward bids the opponent may accept, and their offers suggest they care about keeping issueC and issueD at valueD while they have recently moved on issueB. I therefore concede the lower-weight issueC and issueD while holding issueA and accepting issueB at valueB.

I targeted a still-positive utility around 0.60 by holding firm on my most important issueA while conceding further on issueB and already giving low-utility-to-me values on issueC and issueD. At t=0.34 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s much lower recent offers; their messages claim balance, but the actual bids remain relatively poor for me and only show limited concessions across issues.
